title: NOC 94100 – Machine operators, mineral and metal processing | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s mineral processing sector is a cornerstone of the global "Green Energy" supply chain. Machine Operators (NOC 94100) are the essential workforce transforming raw ores into the critical minerals required for the world’s EV battery revolution. With major investments in Ontario’s "Ring of Fire" and Quebec’s "Battery Valley," this role offers a stable, high-growth entry point for skilled international workers.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Occupations usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Machine operators in mineral and metal processing

duties: Machine Setup: Prepare and adjust mineral ore, metal, and cement processing machinery for specific processing steps.
Operational Oversight: Operate single-function machinery to grind, separate, filter, mix, or refine mineral ores and metals.
Process Monitoring: Observe gauges, computer printouts, and video monitors to verify processing conditions.
Maintenance Assistance: Assist in cleaning and maintaining equipment to prevent production downtime.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Experience: Previous experience as a labourer in mineral and metal processing is usually required for machine operators.
Training: Several weeks of on-the-job training are typically provided by the employer.
Language: CLB 4 or 5 is generally sufficient for most entry-level provincial pathways.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) indicates that while automation may slightly limit new job growth, retirement replacements will account for the majority of job openings. In regions like Quebec's Abitibi-Témiscamingue, the outlook is rated as Very Good due to a localized shortage of experienced operators.
labour_market_demand: The demand for mineral processing operators is shifting toward provinces with significant critical mineral deposits:
Ontario (Northeast Region): High demand in Sudbury and Timmins due to nickel and lithium processing expansion.
Quebec: Very Good prospects in the Abitibi-Témiscamingue region and Good prospects in Chaudière-Appalaches, driven by primary metal manufacturing.
British Columbia: Moderate to Good demand as copper and zinc production scales up for clean-tech exports.
Alberta: Stable prospects within industrial manufacturing supporting the broader energy sector.
Why Work in Canada as a Mineral Machine Operator?
Critical Sector Status: As part of the Federal "Critical Minerals Strategy," these roles are resilient to global economic shifts.
Rapid Career Growth: High potential to transition into Central Control (NOC 93100) or Supervisory roles within 3–5 years.
Comprehensive Benefits: 2026 data shows that over 97% of workers in this NOC receive full medical and dental benefits.
Path to PR: While a TEER 4 role, it is frequently prioritized in specific Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "In-Demand" streams.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): NOC 94100 is explicitly listed as an eligible occupation for the Employer Job Offer: In-Demand Skills Stream.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A direct path for those currently working in Alberta’s industrial or processing sectors.
Express Entry (Trade Category): While NOC 94100 is TEER 4, 2026 category-based draws for Trades have occasionally targeted semi-skilled workers in critical resource sectors.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-success pathway for those willing to work in the Atlantic provinces' refineries and processing plants.
